title: Why AI Ethics Matter
subtitle: AI Ethics & Safety — Like One Academy

sections.text: AI is the most powerful technology humanity has ever built. It can diagnose diseases, write legislation, generate art, and influence elections. And right now, it has almost no rules. In this lesson, you will learn why AI ethics is not a philosophical debate — it is a practical necessity that affects your life today.

Here is why this matters to you personally. AI already decides things about your life. It scores your loan application. It filters your resume before a human sees it. It decides what news you see, what ads you get, what prices you are offered online. If that AI is biased, opaque, or wrong — you pay the price. And most people never even know it happened.

AI ethics breaks down into five core areas. Bias — making sure AI does not discriminate based on race, gender, age, or other protected characteristics. Privacy — controlling how your data is collected, stored, and used. Transparency — knowing when AI is making decisions about you. Accountability — having someone responsible when AI causes harm. And safety — preventing AI from being used in dangerous ways.

leftItems: AI rejects qualified candidates based on zip code
Companies collect your data without consent
No one knows why the AI made that decision
When AI causes harm nobody is responsible
rightItems: AI evaluated on skills regardless of background
Clear consent and data protection for users
Clear chains of responsibility for AI decisions

sections.text: The good news is that you do not need a philosophy degree to engage with AI ethics. As a user, you have power. You can ask companies whether they tested their AI for bias. You can choose tools that are transparent about how they work. You can push back when AI is used to make decisions without human oversight. Ethical AI starts with informed users.

And if you build with AI or use it at work, the stakes are even higher. Deploying biased AI can expose your company to lawsuits. Using AI without transparency erodes customer trust. Collecting data without consent violates regulations in most countries. AI ethics is not just morally right — it is a business imperative.

sections.quote: With great power comes great responsibility. AI is the greatest power we have ever created. The responsibility is ours.

sections.text: In the next lesson, we dive into bias — the most common and most harmful ethical issue in AI. You will learn how bias gets into AI systems, how to spot it, and what you can do about it. This is where ethics gets practical.
